priorities: Capital preservation and steady cash flow
redLines: No exposure to high‑beta speculative asset classes
Avoid US software, AI and mega‑cap technology stocks
No aggressive strategy shifts or volatile growth equities
preferredSectors: Consumer Staples (e.g., Nestlé, Procter & Gamble, Walmart, Costco)
avoidedSectors: US speculative software and cloud technology

investmentBehavior.timeHorizon: Long‑term, multi‑generational focus on preserving wealth for grandchildren
investmentBehavior.liquidity: Low overall; occasional planned withdrawals for family projects and foundation cash‑flow needs

lifeEvents.description: CHF 150,000 withdrawn for a structural renovation of the holiday home in Flims.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Liquidity need met from cash accounts; core equity positions left untouched.

lifeEvents.description: Succession planning discussion; client requested a strict flag to block high‑beta speculative assets.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Reinforced defensive stance and added explicit exclusion rules.

lifeEvents.description: Opened a minor savings account for the oldest grandson to teach traditional saving virtues.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Emphasised long‑term, low‑risk saving habits for the next generation.

lifeEvents.description: Account handover initiated due to Thomas Keller’s early retirement.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Ensured continuity of the global defensive allocation under a new relationship manager.

lifeEvents.description: Official reassignment of the account to RM Sarah Meier.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Maintained the existing conservative mandate with a new point of contact.

lifeEvents.description: Requested all upcoming June bond coupons be swept to the family foundation.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Fixed‑income cash flows directed to charitable activities, confirming income‑generation priority.

keyQuotes: "I want to sleep at night, not speculate on Silicon Valley cloud bubbles."
"These abstract software and AI firms are valued on pure hype. It feels like the dot‑com crash of 2000 all over again."
"We expect the same quiet, global defensive value approach that Thomas Keller provided. No aggressive strategy shifts."
